paragraphs.text: He who had been a consolation to the slaves in Potiphar’s house brought consolation also to the prisoners shut up with him. And in prison he interpreted the two dreams of two of Pharaoh’s servants. And one was hanged on the very day Joseph had foretold him, while to the other, by Joseph’s interpretation, was restored the cup which he was to put into Pharaoh’s hand. Joseph asked the cupbearer to remind Pharaoh of him. But the consequence of his saying, “Remember me” (Gen 40:14), was only that for two years he was forgotten.
